channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: BOEM Explores Potential for OCS Space Launch and Recovery
item.description: 'The Outer Continental Shelf presents a significant opportunity to support the future of America's space economy', Acting BOEM Director Matt Giacona said.

item.title: Venezuela Breaks Up Oil Monopoly Under New Rules
item.description: Venezuela's interim government published long-awaited oil regulations on Thursday.

item.title: Chevron to Continue Supplying Gas to Australian Utility Alinta
item.description: Chevron signed a new agreement to supply 46 petajoules of Western Australian natural gas to Alinta over five years.

item.title: EIA Cuts 2026, 2027 USA Gasoline Price Projection
item.description: The EIA lowered its U.S. regular gasoline price projection for both 2026 and 2027 in its latest short term energy outlook.

item.title: IEA Chief Urges EU to Drop Arctic Drilling Ban
item.description: The European Union should reverse the current moratorium on drilling in the Arctic, where Norway is pushing to drill if allowed, Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency (IEA), has said…

item.title: Strategists Expect USA-Iran Tension to be Short Lived
item.description: Macquarie strategists said they expect the renewed tension in the Middle East between the U.S. and Iran to be 'relatively short-lived'.

item.title: Baker Hughes wins contracts for Cheniere’s Sabine Pass LNG expansion
item.description: Baker Hughes has secured three significant contracts to supply equipment and services for the expansion of Cheniere’s Sabine Pass LNG facility in Cameron Parish, Louisiana, US.

item.title: TotalEnergies exports first cargo from ECA LNG plant to Asia
item.description: TotalEnergies has exported the initial cargo of liquefied natural gas (LNG) produced at the ECA LNG Phase 1 terminal on Mexico’s Pacific Coast to Asia.

item.title: UAE Oil Output Hits All-Time High, Doubling Pre-Crisis Levels
item.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) produced 4.1 million barrels per day (bpd) of crude oil in June, its highest output ever, according to estimates by the International Energy Agency. The UAE’s crude oil production jumped…

item.title: ADNOC, Mitsui Ink Broad Energy Partnership
item.description: ADNOC and Mitsui will explore collaboration in 'crude oil market development and long-term supply, LNG sales and optimization, sulfur procurement and logistics, and shipping solutions for LNG, ammonia, sulfur and other…

item.title: Bureaucracy Is Strangling America's Clean Energy Buildout
item.description: The global energy system is facing a polycrisis. Consecutive and escalating geopolitical crises have thrown the world’s oil and gas markets into turmoil, the artificial intelligence boom is placing unprecedented stress…

item.title: IEA Cuts Russia's Oil Production Forecast Due to Ukrainian Attacks
item.description: Russia's oil production in 2026 and 2027 is set to be lower than previously expected, due to intensified Ukrainian drone attacks on Russia's energy infrastructure, the International Energy Agency said in its monthly oil…

item.title: New Pipeline Paves Way for Increased Israeli Gas Exports to Egypt
item.description: State-owned Israel Natural Gas Lines completed a project to expand the pipeline system that delivers Israeli natural gas to Egypt.

item.title: India’s State Nuclear Firm In Search of Global Uranium Assets
item.description: Indian state-owned NTPC Ltd, the biggest utility in the country, is looking to acquire stakes in uranium assets globally to secure fuel for the expected massive expansion of India’s nuclear power capacity. NTPC…

item.title: India to Expand Crude Reserves
item.description: The board of the country's largest oil-and-gas producer gave the green light to add 1.75 million tons of capacity in Mangalore, Karnataka, to national crude reserves.

item.title: Halliburton deploys LOGIX platform on Eni deepwater well in Indonesia
item.description: Halliburton has deployed its LOGIX automation and remote operations technology on a deepwater exploration well operated by Eni offshore Indonesia.

item.title: Oil Prices Rally on Renewed Hormuz Supply Risks
item.description: August WTI crude oil traded higher during the week ending July 10, with prices last at $71.84 through Thursday evening, up $3.38, or 4.94%. The contract posted a wide weekly range, climbing from a low of $67.82 to a…

item.title: Nord Stream Loses Landmark Insurance Case as War Exclusion Prevails
item.description: Politics, Geopolitics & Conflict A UK High Court ruling has ended Nord Stream AG’s attempt to recover $662 million in insurance claims from the September 2022 sabotage of the Nord Stream 1 gas pipeline, with a judge…

item.title: U.S. Strikes Fail to Break Iran’s Grip on the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. launched another major round of strikes across Iran this week, targeting roughly 90 military sites, including coastal radar installations, anti-ship missile batteries, drone launch positions, command networks…

item.title: ADNOC Orders $900 Million in New LNG Carriers to Expand Global Fleet
item.description: ADNOC Logistics and Services has placed a $900-million order for four newbuild LNG carriers to expand its fleet as Abu Dhabi’s national oil company seeks to boost gas exports to seize the global rise in LNG demand…
